nodes.title: Foundation: hybrid docs + Rust core + Python bridge
nodes.description:
Session epic for worktrees-hives hybrid foundation (start order steps 1–3).

Scope: H3 docs, R1 scaffold+CI, R2 worktrees, R3 state, R5 safe git/gh, H1 JSON contract, P1 Python package+bridge.

Out of scope: R4 supervisor, R6 full status, P2–P4 policy loops (stubs only under P1), full SKILL.md, full packaging script.

Repo: rmems/worktrees-hives (existing clone; no re-clone). Remote origin already configured.
Hard rules: never merge PRs; force-with-lease only; worktree sandbox; no Graphite.
Tracking: beads (session) + GitHub issues + Linear RM project worktrees-hives.
Memory: Ogham + Chroma when using subagents.
nodes.design:
Agent skill → Python orchestrator → Rust wh CLI → git/gh/OS.
IPC: versioned JSON envelopes (schema_version: 1), not PyO3.
Git backend: subprocess git, not libgit2.
State: ~/.local/share/worktrees-hives/watched.json (XDG).
Worktrees: ~/.local/share/worktrees-hives/worktrees/{owner}/{repo}/{job_id}.
nodes.acceptance:
- README/AGENTS/REVIEW codify hybrid layers
- wh-core + wh build; rust CI green
- worktree create/list/remove + sandbox tests
- atomic state store + wh state *
- safe git/gh block merge and bare force
- docs/json-contract.md + dual-side fixture tests
- installable Python package + WhClient + mocked tests
- PR open (never auto-merged); GH/Linear linked

nodes.title: H3: Codify hybrid design in README, AGENTS, REVIEW
nodes.description:
Maps GH#42 (dup #36) / #20 / #3 partial.

Expand operator docs for Python/Rust hybrid: architecture table, safety hard rules, build/install for wh + Python package, planned command surface ownership.

Also: epic #1 body hybrid note; link Linear project.
nodes.design:
Files: README.md, AGENTS.md, REVIEW.md (new).
Layers: Agent skill vs Python policy vs Rust hard-stops.
No full SKILL.md (#2) this bead.
nodes.acceptance:
- README architecture + safety + build wh + pip install path
- AGENTS.md layer responsibilities + data flow
- REVIEW.md human/agent checklist with Rust and Python notes
- Links to #21–#23 epics and docs/json-contract.md (when H1 lands)
- No auto-merge language anywhere

nodes.title: R1: Cargo workspace scaffold (wh-core + wh) and CI
nodes.description:
Maps GH#24.

Bootstrap Cargo workspace: wh-core lib + wh bin; rustfmt/clippy/test CI; README build section; MSRV stable.
nodes.design:
crates/wh-core, crates/wh; clap CLI; exit 0/1/2 (policy).
Modules placeholders: error, paths, contract, worktree, state, git_safe.
nodes.acceptance:
- Cargo.toml workspace members wh-core + wh
- cargo test / clippy -D warnings / fmt green
- .github/workflows/rust.yml
- README build & install wh
- MSRV documented

nodes.title: H1: Versioned JSON contract between Python and Rust
nodes.description:
Maps GH#40 (dup #34).

Stable IPC: request/response JSON envelopes with schema_version, shared fixtures, validation tests on both sides.
nodes.design:
Envelope: {ok, schema_version, command, data, error}.
Commands v1: worktree.*, state.*, git.run, gh.run; status minimal; run-with-timeout reserved for R4.
Compatibility: additive fields OK; renames need version bump.
Artifacts: docs/json-contract.md, docs/examples/*.json, wh-core::contract, worktrees_hives.contract.
nodes.acceptance:
- Documented operations + examples
- schema_version field + compatibility policy
- Fixture validation in Rust and Python
- wh --json emits envelopes

nodes.title: R3: Job/state store (serde JSON, atomic write)
nodes.description:
Maps GH#26 / planning #12.

Persistent hive job state under XDG data dir with atomic replace.
nodes.design:
watched.json v1; fields: repo, number, kind, branch, worktree_path, stack_id, status, fix_count, residual_blockers, timestamps.
Atomic: write temp + rename. Override WH_STATE_PATH.
nodes.acceptance:
- Schema as designed
- Load/save atomic replace
- Default path ~/.local/share/worktrees-hives/watched.json
- CLI wh state show|add|remove (+ --json)
- Unit tests round-trip + empty store

nodes.title: R2: Worktree manager (create/list/cleanup, path sandbox)
nodes.description:
Maps GH#25 / planning #6.

Isolated git worktrees for hive jobs with path sandboxing.
nodes.design:
Base WH_WORKTREE_BASE or ~/.local/share/worktrees-hives/worktrees.
Path: {base}/{owner}/{repo}/{job_id}.
Reject .. and path escape. git worktree via subprocess.
nodes.acceptance:
- Create worktree for branch at configured base
- List hive worktrees
- Cleanup/remove
- Reject paths outside sandbox
- Unit tests with temp git repos
- JSON envelopes

nodes.title: R5: Safe git/gh wrappers (block merge and bare force)
nodes.description:
Maps GH#28 / planning #13.

Hard safety: dangerous git/gh ops rejected in Rust even if Python/skill mis-prompts.
nodes.design:
Allowlisted git; force only --force-with-lease; reject gh pr merge and bare force; branch verify vs job/CLI; ErrorCode set matching contract.
gh api denied by default in v1.
nodes.acceptance:
- Allowlisted git subcommands
- Force push only with --force-with-lease
- Reject gh pr merge
- Branch mismatch blocks mutations
- Structured error codes (exit 2)
- Unit tests for force/merge/branch policy

nodes.title: P1: Python package + subprocess bridge to wh CLI
nodes.description:
Maps GH#30 / epic #23 start.

Installable Python package that treats Rust wh as system of record for worktrees/state/safe git.
Include stub modules for P2–P4 (discover, partition, issue_pr, babysit, report) raising NotImplementedError with issue refs.
nodes.design:
python/pyproject.toml; package worktrees_hives.
WhClient: WH_BIN / PATH lookup; --json; PolicyError on exit 2.
Stubs only for policy loops — not full P2–P4.
nodes.acceptance:
- Installable under python/ (pip install -e)
- Thin WhClient invoke wh with JSON stdout
- Clear WhNotFoundError if wh missing
- Smoke tests with mocked subprocess
- Stub modules for discover/partition/issue_pr/babysit/report
- pytest CI job

nodes.title: Open foundation PR (never merge) + cross-link GH/Linear
nodes.description:
After implementation: push feature branch, open PR via GitKraken/gh, comment on GH issues #24/#25/#26/#28/#30/#40/#42 and Linear RM mirrors. Never auto-merge.

Session tooling: beads close children; Ogham store decisions; Chroma log PR URL.
nodes.acceptance:
- Feature branch pushed
- PR open with checklist mapping R1–R3,R5,P1,H1,H3
- GH + Linear comments with PR URL
- No merge performed by agent
